methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Xaira Therapeutics, a seed-stage biotech company founded by Marc Tessier-Lavigne, has raised $1.0B to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 11 peers (3 public: RXRX, TEM, ONT.L; 8 private including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics), though narrow-filter peer matching proved insufficient for robust median-based triangulation.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($1.0B) as a baseline value proxy and applying a standard 2.5x multiplier for seed-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $2.5B. This multiplier reflects typical step-up dynamics as companies progress from seed toward Series A. Public-comparable anchoring (median P/S of 7.8x across three biotech tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data, common among private companies. Similarly, peer-median methods lack sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with disclosed valuations to meet analytical thresholds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.1B to $2.9B, centered at $2.5B. This reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of actual revenue or near-term financial metrics, reliance on a single methodological input, and limited disclosure among the identified private peer set. Validation would benefit from fundraising round terms, burn trajectory, and clinical or product development milestones not present in available data.
